belief: TFT early economy: save gold above 50 for interest in early game (stage 2-1 to 3-2)
abstract_policy: In early to mid game (stages 2-3), prioritize maintaining 50 gold for maximum interest income. Spend only excess gold above 50.
dos: field strong board with interest gold

belief: TFT mid-game transition: evaluate board strength before commiting to a comp (stage 3-2 to 4-5)
goal: smooth transition into late game composition
abstract_policy: At stage 3-2 to 4-5, assess whether current board can survive. If losing streak, commit to a flexible comp and roll at level 7.
donts: commit too early to an expensive comp
save gold while taking 15+ damage per round

belief: TFT late game: level to 8/9 before rolling for 4-cost carries (stage 4-2+)
abstract_policy: After stage 4-2, prioritize reaching level 8 before heavy rolling. Level 9 only if high HP (>40) and stable board.

belief: TFT HP management: play conservatively when below 30 HP, prioritize survival
goal: maximize placement by avoiding early elimination
abstract_policy: When below 30 HP, spend all excess gold to stabilize. Prioritize strong 2-star units over econ. Every round matters.
dos: roll to zero if below 20 HP
field strongest board regardless of econ
prioritize AOE units to stop losing streak
donts: save gold when at risk of elimination
pivot to a completely new comp

belief: TFT item priority: slam strong items early, dont hold components for perfect items
goal: maximize immediate board power through items
abstract_policy: Slam items early (stage 2-1 to 3-2) rather than holding components for perfect items. Early power spike preserves HP and win streak.
dos: use components even if not BIS
donts: refuse to build because waiting for BIS

belief: TFT streak management: maintain win or loss streaks for max gold income
abstract_policy: If win streaking, level aggressively to maintain streak. If loss streaking, open fort to preserve econ and guarantee first carousel pick.
dos: sell field if loss streaking for econ
intentional loss streak needs end condition

belief: TFT rolling: roll only at level thresholds (6/7/8/9) for specific cost units
goal: efficient gold spending for unit upgrade probability
abstract_policy: Roll at level 6 for 3-cost carries, level 7 for 3-cost 3-star, level 8 for 4-cost 2-star. Never roll at level 5 unless specific 1-cost reroll comp.

belief: TFT augment choice: personal augment beats combat augment in early game
goal: optimal augment selection for current board state
abstract_policy: Economic augments (Thief's Gloves+, Level Up, etc.) scale better long term. Combat augments are for immediate power. Hero augments only if you have the carry.
dos: pick econ augment if strong board
avoid hero augments without the unit

belief: TFT positioning: spread carries in late game to avoid AOE wipes
abstract_policy: Position tanks in front 2 rows, carries back row corners. Spread carries vs AOE. Bait units with isolated tank. Scout opponents before each round.
dos: put tank in corner to bait assassins
donts: put main carry in direct path of enemy assassin

belief: TFT scouting: check opponents boards every round after stage 3
goal: gain advantage by adapting to opponents
abstract_policy: After stage 3, scout 1-2 strongest opponents each round. Adapt positioning and unit choices based on contested units.
dos: scout top 2 opponents before each round
donts: build same comp as 3 other players

belief: TFT flexible play: never force a comp, adapt to what the game gives you
abstract_policy: Let natural unit drops guide your comp. Force 1-cost reroll only if you natural 3+ copies by 2-1. Pivot out of contested comps.
dos: pick first augment based on items
pivot if 3+ players contest your comp
donts: force the same comp every game
commit to AP comp with AD items

belief: TFT win condition: identify your win condition by stage 4-1
goal: set clear path to top 4 placement
abstract_policy: By stage 4-1, you should know if you're playing for top 1 (highroll), top 4 (stable), or top 6 (salvage). Adjust spending accordingly.
